key_insight: A comprehensive hybrid-DFT database maps spin-polarized electronic structure and bonding across 2,500+ stable Cs2BB'X6 halide double perovskites, revealing many visible-gap and half-metal candidates.
engineering_relevance: The dataset accelerates screening and design of halide double perovskites for photovoltaics, photocatalysis, radiation detection, and spintronic or magnetic applications.
method: Calculated DOS for >2,500 predicted-stable compounds using hybrid-functional DFT.
Focused on Cs2BB'X6 compositions with X = I, Br, Cl, and F.
Added LOBSTER bonding analysis plus UMAP visualizations and an interactive exploration app.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 719 visible-range band-gap compounds and 118 half-metallic materials, indicating broad functional diversity in the screened dataset.
watch_item: Stability is based on the τ tolerance factor screening, so predicted candidates still need experimental validation before device use.
paper_title: Spin-Polarized Electronic Structure and Chemical Bonding Data for 2,500+ Halide Double Perovskites
